refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation with communication quality meeting the customer-defined packet loss limit.
refined_fr.rationale: The candidate FR focuses on a single obligation: ensuring teleoperation communication quality meets a specified packet loss limit. It is already solution-neutral, specifying 'communication quality' without prescribing how to achieve it, and it is atomic as it addresses one clear functional requirement.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the packet loss rate during teleoperation under agreed network conditions and verify it does not exceed the customer-defined limit.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation.
The NGHE solution shall ensure communication quality meets the customer-defined packet loss limit during teleoperation.

candidate_dps: Implement a communication protocol with adaptive error correction and retransmission mechanisms to maintain packet loss within limits
Design a network quality monitoring and control system that dynamically adjusts transmission parameters to meet packet loss targets
Use a redundant communication path strategy to ensure packet loss stays below the threshold
Incorporate a buffering and smoothing mechanism at the receiver to compensate for packet loss and maintain teleoperation quality
selected_dp.selected_dp: Design a network quality monitoring and control system that dynamically adjusts transmission parameters to meet packet loss targets

potential_sources: Tight coupling between monitoring and control functions if not modularized
Dependency on specific network conditions or hardware implementations
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP logically separates monitoring and control functions, allowing independent adjustment of parameters to meet packet loss requirements. This supports modularity and reduces coupling.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network conditions can be monitored accurately in real-time
Assumes control adjustments can be made without violating operational policies
Risk that network variability exceeds control system capability leading to packet loss above target
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly defining interfaces between monitoring and control modules to further reduce coupling and improve modularity.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses network-related functionalities including monitoring and control of network parameters to ensure quality of service.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Used to receive network performance data and send monitoring reports.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Interface to adjust transmission parameters and communicate with network hardware.

module_containment_scores.notes: Highly cohesive as network quality monitoring and control logically belong here; minimal diffusion expected outside this module.
Vehicle module is focused on vehicle-specific functions; network control would diffuse significantly outside.
Teleoperation depends on network but does not own network control; moderate diffusion expected.
Safety Services focus on safety alerts, not network control; poor containment and high diffusion.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most appropriate boundary to contain the network quality monitoring and control DP, as it specializes in network-related functions and can encapsulate the logic and control mechanisms with minimal diffusion.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on Telemetry interface may require coordination with Teleoperation module.
Adjusting transmission parameters might impact vehicle or teleoperation performance, requiring careful interface management.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP logically addresses the FR by specifying a network quality monitoring and control system that dynamically adjusts transmission parameters to meet the packet loss targets defined by the customer. This directly supports the teleoperation requirement with the specified communication quality.

diffusion_risks: Potential dependency on Telemetry interface may require coordination with Teleoperation module.
Adjusting transmission parameters might impact vehicle or teleoperation performance, requiring careful interface management.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module shows high containment (score 9) and low diffusion (score 2), indicating that the DP is well encapsulated within this IM boundary. Other modules show poor containment and higher diffusion scores, supporting the choice of NGHE Solution as the ownership candidate.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible through monitoring packet loss metrics via the Telemetry interface and validating that packet loss remains within the customer-defined limit under agreed network conditions.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the DP to the NGHE Solution IM is clear and appropriate, as this module encompasses the network-related functionalities necessary to realize the FR.

recommended_revisions: Explicitly document interface coordination protocols between NGHE Solution and Teleoperation modules to manage dependencies and avoid unintended impacts.
Include monitoring and mitigation strategies for potential side effects on vehicle and teleoperation performance due to transmission parameter adjustments.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed successfully with strong justification and verification feasibility. The DP logically addresses the FR, the IM module (NGHE Solution) shows high containment and low diffusion, and verification methods are clear. Although there are some diffusion risks and recommended revisions, these relate to interface coordination and monitoring strategies rather than fundamental decomposition issues. Given the high safety criticality, it is prudent to finalize this level before proceeding to sub-FRs to ensure stability and clarity in the current design.

revise.why: To explicitly document interface coordination protocols between NGHE Solution and Teleoperation modules and include monitoring and mitigation strategies for potential side effects on vehicle and teleoperation performance due to transmission parameter adjustments.

assumptions: The NGHE Solution module can encapsulate the DP effectively with minimal diffusion.
Telemetry interface provides reliable packet loss metrics for verification.
open_risks: Potential dependency on Telemetry interface requiring coordination with Teleoperation module.
Adjusting transmission parameters might impact vehicle or teleoperation performance, requiring careful interface management.
evidence_needed: Verification data showing packet loss remains within customer-defined limits under operational conditions.
Documentation of interface coordination protocols and mitigation strategies.
